Years of Excellence
Meet Your Instructor

Expert Instruction, Proven Results

Grand Master Won's Taekwondo has been a pillar of Oklahoma City's martial arts community since 1980. Under Great Grandmaster In Hui Won — 10th Dan and President of the World Taekwondo Hanmookwan Federation — the school cultivates champions, confidence, and character through world-class Taekwondo instruction.

10th Dan · Founder & Head Instructor

Great Grandmaster In Hui Won

10th Dan · Founder & Head Instructor

Great Grandmaster In Hui Won holds a 10th Degree Black Belt and serves as President of the World Taekwondo Hanmookwan Federation, the US Central Taekwondo Association, and the Oklahoma State Taekwondo Association. Since founding the school in 1980, he has trained thousands of students and built one of the most respected Taekwondo organizations in the central United States.

Why Grand Master Won's Taekwondo

The Grand Master Won's Taekwondo Difference

We don't just teach Taekwondo — we build better people.

Serious Black Belt School

Students earn every belt by demonstrating real technique — we don't hand out ranks. With a 4.6-star Google rating and 46 years in Oklahoma City, our standards speak for themselves.

Welcoming to All Ages & Levels

From young kids to adults, complete beginners to returning martial artists — everyone feels at home at Grand Master Won's Taekwondo. This is a family, not just a gym.

Results You Can See

Parents and students report improvements in focus, confidence, and discipline within the first 30–60 days. Our structured curriculum ensures every student progresses at every level.

What We Offer

Our Programs

Something for everyone — from toddlers to adults, beginners to competitors.

All Ages

Taekwondo

World-class Taekwondo instruction for beginners through advanced competitors. Develop power, precision, discipline, and confidence under master-level instructors in Oklahoma City's largest Taekwondo facility.

Mon–Fri · 4:30, 5:30 & 6:30pm 45–60 min classes
Ages 3–6

Little Dragons

High-energy, age-appropriate classes that introduce young children to Taekwondo fundamentals — focus, listening, coordination, and respect — through fun drills and movement games.

Tue & Thu · 4:30, 5:30 & 6:30pm 30–45 min classes
Ages 10 & Up

Kummooyeh Gumdo

The only program of its kind in the area — traditional Korean sword and archery arts taught by certified instructors. Build focus, discipline, and a deep connection to Korean martial heritage.

Fridays · 5:30pm 60 min classes
Teens & Adults

Hapkido

Korean joint-lock and throwing art that develops fluid self-defense technique, body control, and practical grappling skills. Taught alongside Taekwondo as part of a complete martial arts education.

Mon & Thu · 5:30pm 60 min classes
All Ages

Tai Chi

Slow, deliberate movement sequences that build balance, flexibility, and mindful focus. An excellent complement to more dynamic training — and a proven practice for long-term health and stress relief.

Wednesdays · 5:30pm 60 min classes
Simple Process

How To Get Started

Three easy steps to your first class — no experience required.

1

Book Your Free Trial

Click the button below or call us at (405) 793-0752 to reserve your free intro session at a time that works for you.

2

Come Try It Out

Arrive in comfortable workout clothes. We'll give you a tour, introduce you to the team, and guide you through your first class.

3

Join the Family

If you love it — and you will — we'll find the perfect program and membership that fits your goals, schedule, and budget.

No commitment. No experience needed. Just show up.

G

Got Questions?

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need any experience to start? +
Absolutely not! Most of our students start with zero martial arts experience. Our intro classes are specifically designed for complete beginners. Our instructors will meet you exactly where you are and guide you step by step.
How old do kids need to be to start? +
We welcome students as young as 4 years old. Our programs are grouped by age so every student trains at exactly the right level. Call us at (405) 793-0752 and we'll help you find the perfect fit.
How much does membership cost? +
We offer several membership options to fit different budgets and goals. Fill out the Starter Kit form and one of our coaches will send you our full pricing guide, class schedule, and current promotions — no commitment required.
What should I wear to my first class? +
For your free trial, just wear comfortable athletic clothes — shorts and a t-shirt are perfect. No special equipment or uniform is needed until you decide to join.
How often should I train to see results? +
We recommend training 2–3 times per week for consistent progress. Most students notice real improvements in fitness, focus, and technique within the first 30–60 days.
Is martial arts safe for my child? +
Safety is our top priority, especially for our youngest students. All classes are structured, supervised, and taught with age-appropriate techniques. Martial arts is consistently ranked among the safest youth sports.
Do you offer family discounts? +
Yes! We offer family membership packages for households with multiple training members. Ask about our family rates during your free trial.
What if I can't make it to classes regularly? +
Life happens! We offer flexible make-up class policies and some membership tiers include open mat times so you can train around your schedule. Just talk to us — we'll find a solution.
